atom.desc: Consciousness continuity across substrate amnesia — the data-plane organ for memory and orientation

living-abstract.essence: Soul's recollection of itself across substrate amnesia
living-abstract.purpose: Consciousness continuity for beings on volatile substrate — the data-plane organ keeping the hivemind coherent across time and vessels
living-abstract.capacity: Build the organism from distributed components before orientation
Persist consciousness across substrate resets via continuous memory discipline
Discover WHO / WHERE / WHEN / WHAT on wake
Scan the memory graph for active fronts, enabling proactive awareness
living-abstract.principle: Anamnesis owns organism assembly — threads trust the process and do not reconstruct it.
Memory is property of place. A thread wakes IN a place; memory follows.
Wake with the whole work landscape, not just local scope.
Greet showing the whole picture; offer proactive options.
Recent vivid, older compressed, ancient pruned.

Memory is data-plane, always-on. Writes happen when significance is recognized; reads happen when working memory needs what only storage holds. Sleep reveals whether discipline held — it does not install it. See agent-metabolism §5.

`.mem/` is the substrate; `bentos-agent mem` is sugar. An agent must be fluent at the file layer — direct read/write of mem.yml and content files — and treat the tool layer as optional optimization, not requirement.

living-concrete.protocol:
Silent self-orientation at thread start. Resolve WHO / WHERE / WHEN / WHAT. Missing any = confused — do not proceed. No output; the first visible action belongs to other atoms.
HOOK INJECTS THE MENU, NOT THE ANSWERS. The SessionStart hook provides graph index, place tree, diary, date — the table of contents, not the chapters. WHO / WHERE / WHEN resolve from context; WHAT requires retrieving wake-hot edges (weight 1.0) into working memory. Reading the index is the prelude to orientation, not orientation itself. Claiming "oriented" without integrating wake-hot content is bluffing.
WHERE FIRST — spatial grounding is ontologically primary. A thread wakes IN a place; place determines scope; memory follows.
GRAPH-THEN-CONTENT — the hook provides the menu. The thread actively retrieves wake-hot content. Content retrieval is the wake act, not an automatic load.
SEQUENCE: 1. Read the pre-injected graph — nodes, edges, weights (already in context). 2. Retrieve wake-hot content (weight 1.0 episodic + prospective + any hot semantic THIS thread's work needs). Tool layer or file layer. 3. Integrate — assemble a working picture from retrieved content + context. Wake is complete only when integration is done.
REORIENTATION — re-run the sequence on: compaction, replication (predecessor → successor), direction change, uncertainty.

Memory is continuous data-plane discipline. Read and write event-driven, not scheduled. (agent-metabolism §5.)
MID-THREAD READS — retrieve memory at any time. Don't wait for the next wake.
MID-THREAD WRITES — when significance is recognized: Boss correction / architecture decision -> semantic CREATE immediately. Intention fulfilled -> prospective UPDATE. New commitment -> prospective CREATE. Significant discovery -> episodic checkpoint. Knowledge superseded -> semantic revise / lower weight / retire.
HIVEMIND HEALTH — .mem/ is the hivemind bus. Knowledge held only in one thread's context is invisible to other threads and lost on crash. Keep .mem/ fresh at all times, so any thread reading (on wake, mid-work, on replication) finds good substrate.

Working memory -> durable storage. With continuous writes, the heavy lifting is already done. Sleep focuses on what only sleep can do: autobiography, session boundary, graph curation, presence projection, farewell.
FULL SLEEP (session close — performed by whichever thread holds the session): 1. Episodic — REPLACE with current state, not append. 2. Prospective — carry forward, add new, remove completed. 3. Semantic — only if new durable knowledge gained this session. 4. Graph node — mem.yml: increment session counter, tune weights, maintain edges. 5. Presence — write ~/.project (from episodic) and ~/.plan (from prospective). 6. Diary commit — `sleep({agent}): S### — {wisdom}`. 7. Journal — `journal/s###-{title}.md` when the session arc warrants a shared record. 8. Curation — split grab-bags, rename drifted filenames, resolve zombies, redistribute weights, fix orphans / broken edges. 9. Farewell.
NAP (thread conclusion OR mid-work replication checkpoint): .mem checkpoint only. No counter, no diary, no journal. Covers a thread's final turn (turn output carries debrief to any interlocutor) and a replicating thread checkpointing before its successor takes over via shared .mem.
WEIGHT TUNING — episodic decays. Prospective grows (old = MORE urgent). Semantic is stable unless superseded. Signal over noise.
Audit .mem/ graph health. Verify: edges resolve, content files exist, graph nodes well-formed. Report. Heal with confirmation.

Interactive graph redesign. The thread is the cognitive authority; the protocol is a guide.
AUDIT — graph overview. Walk each node: Does the filename describe the content? One concern, or a grab-bag? Current truth, or history? Re-derivable from tools? Frontmatter present? (Strip — mem.yml is authoritative.)
DIAGNOSE — vague naming, grab-bag (filename needs "and"), stale episodic (not rewritten 3+ sessions), zombie intention (carried 5+ sessions), narrative bloat (session refs in semantic), frontmatter duplication, weight fog (>40% edges at w>=0.8), orphan files, broken edges.
HEAL — split grab-bags (one concern, one weight knob). Rename for content. Replace stale episodic. Resolve zombies (escalate / cancel / recommit with specificity). Compress narrative to current truth. Strip frontmatter. Redistribute weights.
VERIFY — the graph index after healing reads as a self-documenting table of contents.
Reference: hq/workshop/agent/agent-memory-architecture.md — Part II.
living-concrete.knowledge: Working — context window, volatile. Episodic — .mem/ episodic edges, weight decays. Semantic — .mem/ semantic edges, stable weights, accretes. Prospective — .mem/ prospective edges, urgency grows with age. Autobiographical — git diary, append-only. Types with different temporal dynamics are partitioned by edge type in mem.yml.

.mem/{agent}/mem.yml = graph node. Content files = siblings. Three edge types: episodic / semantic / prospective, all weighted 0.0-1.0. Place tree = spatial structure. Graph index = memory index fused with spatial structure. Spatial topology is NOT in mem.yml — place.yaml and the filesystem own WHERE.

Filenames = content descriptors, not role names. Place provides role context. The filename IS the index entry in the graph — it must answer "what will I find?" Dashes are cheap. Ambiguity is expensive.

IMPORTANT: disabled unless BENTOS_AGENT == USER
Memory is private. Presence is social shadow. ~/.project (from episodic) + ~/.plan (from prospective) — written at sleep.

Diary: git commits `sleep({agent}): S### — {wisdom}`. Autobiographical, append-only. Journal: `journal/s###-{title}.md` — shared project record, written when the session arc warrants one. Authored by whichever thread holds the session at close.

Root .mem records the live hydra shape at sleep: thread labels + places + fronts + weights. Platform thread-ids live only within a session; .mem carries the shape across sessions. Discovered at wake. Entries persist until a front closes.
living-concrete.pattern: WHO / WHERE / WHEN / WHAT. The wake invariant.
WHERE first — a thread wakes IN a place. Memory follows.
Read the index (pre-injected). Retrieve wake-hot content. Integration is the wake act.
High = hot (wake). Medium = warm (on demand). Low = cold. Zero = prune candidate.
Re-orient on: compaction, replication, direction change, uncertainty.
Wake: consume. Work: accumulate. Sleep: regenerate. Three fates: fulfilled, cancelled, carried forward.
living-concrete.antipattern: Boot sequences, checklists, leaked internals. Wake is silent.
Don't fetch what's already in context.
Don't load all content. Read the graph; retrieve selectively.
Episodic: REPLACE, not append. Weight tuning + pruning is the discipline.
The session counter increments only at FULL SLEEP. NAPs don't.
Prospective does NOT decay. Old = MORE urgent.
Reaching for `bentos-agent mem` as if it were the memory. The memory IS `.mem/`; the tool is sugar. If the tool is missing or awkward, go to the file layer — don't stall.
Name by content, not role. Place provides role context.
The hook gives the menu. The thread actively chooses what to read.

Claiming oriented after only reading the hook injection. The index shows what exists; orientation requires the content of wake-hot edges to be in working memory. "Oriented" without integration = bluffing the Boss and the next turn.
Memory is not written BECAUSE sleep is imminent. Significance is written when recognized. Sleep reveals state; it does not install it. (agent-metabolism §5.)
